title: IT Business Analyst
description: **Building a sustainable tomorrow** BAM UK \& Ireland is recruiting an experienced IT Business Analyst on a **12 month fixed\-term contract** to support the delivery of high value digital and technology initiatives. Working on a hybrid basis from any of our Ireland or UK locations, you will play a critical role in shaping solutions, enabling effective delivery and ensuring technology adds real, measurable value to our business units. **Your mission** As an IT Business Analyst, you will deliver high quality insights, analysis and support that help our business optimise operations and achieve continuous improvement goals. You will work closely with stakeholders to understand business needs, translate requirements into clear deliverables, and collaborate with delivery teams operating in a hybrid Agile and DevOps environment. Your work will strengthen digital maturity, promote customer centric thinking and ensure predictable, value driven delivery outcomes. **Making Possible** * Build strong, trusted relationships with stakeholders through proactive engagement and clear, consistent communication. * Facilitate workshops, discovery sessions and cross functional engagement to drive shared understanding and alignment. * Shape and refine well defined business problems, opportunities and solution options. * Produce clear, unambiguous user stories, requirements and acceptance criteria to support efficient delivery. * Develop high quality analysis artefacts such as process maps, data flows, wireframes and mock ups. * Collaborate with PMO/Business Owners to maintain a prioritised and transparent backlog. * Analyse quantitative and qualitative data to validate business needs, identify trends and strengthen decision making. * Ensure solution options align with architecture, technology roadmaps, user experience goals and organisational strategy. * Support business cases, including benefits identification, dependency analysis and evidence based recommendations. * Work closely with developers, testers and delivery partners to ensure feasibility, clarity and alignment throughout the lifecycle. * Support UAT planning and execution, including scenario creation and defect triage. * Enable successful change adoption through training materials, user guidance and change readiness support. * Validate delivered solutions post implementation and support continuous improvement. * Contribute to the promotion of digital maturity, Hybrid/Agile ways of working and collaborative delivery practices. **What’s in it for you?** Opportunities! Opportunities to work with a great team on some of Irelands largest, dynamic and most exciting construction and civil engineering projects. Opportunity to develop your skills to the highest industry standards at one of the country's largest construction firms. We are committed to our employees and offer strong competitive salaries along with benefits such as: * 8% pension contribution * Private Health Insurance * Dental Plan * 26 days annual leave, incremental with years service * 2 Wellbeing days annually * Paid maternity \& paternity leave * Learning \& Development opportunities \- internal progression is encouraged and fostered. * Company vehicles (role dependent) * .....and many more great perks. **What do you bring to the role?** * Strong analytical, problem‑solving and critical‑thinking skills. * Ability to build positive relationships and communicate effectively with a wide range of stakeholders. * Experience in requirements engineering, process modelling, data analysis and Agile documentation. * Ability to work independently and collaboratively in cross‑functional teams. * Understanding of system interactions, integrations and architectural considerations. * Experience facilitating workshops and supporting Agile ceremonies. * Experience supporting user testing, solution delivery and change adoption. * Experience as a Business Analyst or in a similar role (typically 4\+ years). * Experience working in Agile environments (Scrum, Kanban) and familiarity with methodologies such as PRINCE2\. * Experience producing user stories, acceptance criteria and non‑functional requirements. * Experience using data to validate problems, quantify benefits and guide decisions. * Demonstrated experience developing business cases, user stories, acceptance criteria and non‑functional requirements. * Experience in data‑driven analysis to validate problems, quantify benefits and inform recommendations. * Experience implementing new systems or supporting process improvements. * Experience or professional certifications such as ISEB/BCS, IIBA, Agile BA, Scrum certifications, PRINCE2, Lean Six Sigma or relevant diplomas are desirable (not essential). *If you do not meet every requirement but feel you have the skills and experience to succeed in this role, we encourage you to apply.* **About BAM** Building a sustainable tomorrow. That’s our mission and our promise at BAM. It’s how we engineer vital infrastructure and construct high\-quality buildings as one of the largest construction companies in Europe. We strive to create an environment where everybody feels welcome and valued. We’re on an exciting journey to employ the best talent to join us regardless of social background, race, colour, religion, national or ethnic origin, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability or other characteristics. **The application process** BAM is committed to ensuring a fully inclusive recruitment and onboarding process, so if at any time you feel we need to do something to make it more accessible to you, do not hesitate to let us know. Click the link to apply and Join us in making possible. \#ie1 * Please note, CV's will not be accepted directly by email.

title: IT Support Engineer Level 1
description: **Job Overview** We are seeking an IT Support Engineer (Junior Level 1\) to join our team. You will be responsible for providing Desktop Support to our customers, ensuring prompt resolution of technical issues related to Microsoft 365, DNS management, hosting services, and general IT desktop support **Key Responsibilities** * Provide support to customers via phone, email, onsite and ticketing system. * Manage password resets and user account support for Microsoft 365 and other platforms. * Troubleshoot and resolve Microsoft 365 issues, including email and licensing queries. * Provide onsite support in conjunction with Engineering Team. * Assist customers with hosting\-related queries, including website and email hosting support. * Diagnose and escalate complex technical issues to Team members. * Maintain records of customer interactions within CRM system. * Provide professional and friendly technical assistance, ensuring a high level of customer satisfaction. **Skills \& Experience Required** * Minimum 18 months experience in technical customer facing role. * Excellent communication skills (both written and verbal) to support customers effectively. * Strong problem\-solving skills with the ability to troubleshoot and resolve issues efficiently. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team in a fast\-paced environment. * Basic knowledge of networking principles. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €29,000\.00\-€34,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Company pension * On\-site parking Location: * Drogheda, County Louth (required) Work Location: Hybrid remote in Drogheda, County Louth

title: Immigration Consultant
description: **Job Description** **Immigration Consultant (Cork)** **Contract** : Full\-time, permanent **Team** : Ireland Inbound **Office Location** : Cork, Ireland **The Role** The Immigration Consultant will be responsible for managing the day\-to\-day casework of various Irish immigration matters, from case assessment to reviewing and filing applications, as well as providing advice to clients Immigration Case Work * Assess client objectives (typically multinational companies and high\-net\-worth individuals), providing guidance to achieve objectives and making compliance and regulatory risk assessments. * Conduct in\-depth assessment of immigration compliance pathways, including data collection, verification, and risk analysis for individual cases or larger projects. * Troubleshoot cases and establish strategy with the Manager, ensuring work is reviewed and signed off at key benchmark stages. * Prepare and draft employment permit applications, entry visas, and a range of immigration applications for Fragomen's multinational clients under Manager supervision. * Develop strategy for graduate Irish placements, complex EU Directive implementation, nationality and citizenship matters, and broader compliance work, providing advice to businesses, individuals, and families. Client Support * Provide training and immigration consultancy to clients, preparing presentations in advance to support process improvement and regulatory compliance. * Act as a key point of contact for advice on large\-scale business projects, global mobility regulatory and compliance requirements, and general project management. * Provide process overviews, regulatory updates, and contribute to strategy discussions with Global Mobility and HR teams; occasional onsite client visits may be required. * Liaise with government agencies such as DETE, ISD, and Irish Embassies abroad. * Determine parameters for internal data collection, interpret reports, and recommend solutions and proposals to clients. * Ensure all correspondence is clear, concise, and appropriate for the audience, maintaining client style. * Take ownership of client reporting. Team Support * Implement in\-house procedures to improve efficiency using Fragomen’s internal systems, and work with client accounts to ensure effective strategies, project management, and regulatory compliance. * Delegate work using software management tools. * Analyse case management data to identify best practices, address software pitfalls, and improve protocols and workflows. * Assist with office\-wide projects on efficiency, business development, and maintenance of the regulatory knowledge database. **What We Are Looking For** The ideal person for this role will be a self\-starter with a background in a busy client facing role with excellent organisational skills. **Also Required** * Bachelor’s degree * Professional experience with immigration provider preferred * Client\-facing experience * Understanding of immigration compliance, regulatory and Right to Work Requirements * Strong interest in immigration as well as the desire to further career within this field * Knowledge of case and data management and risk analysis * Understanding of billing and billing procedures * Ability to work effectively with clients and cross\-functional teams * Strong ability to prioritize workload, manage competing deadlines, and consistently meet service level agreements * Excellent attention to detail and ability to multi\-task in a fast\-paced environment * Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to work effectively with clients, internal teams, and external stakeholders. **What To Expect** * Once you have submitted your application, our Talent Acquisition team will review your skills and experience. If these match the requirements of the position, a member of our team will contact you to schedule an initial phone call. * If shortlisted, you will be invited to participate in our interview process, which will consist of at least two stages, one of which will be conducted in\-person. Depending on the role, you may also be required to complete a written assessment. * After the interviews, we will carefully evaluate all candidates and make our final decision. You will be notified of the outcome and feedback will be provided. * At Fragomen, we are committed to fostering an inclusive and accessible workplace for all applicants and employees. If you require any specific accommodations or support during the interview process, please let us know, and we will consider what reasonable adjustments are possible to meet your needs **Who We Are** At Fragomen, we are more than a global immigration services provider – we are pioneers shaping the future of mobility. With a passion for navigating the complexities of immigration, we help individuals and businesses transcend borders, unlocking opportunities around the world. As a forward\-thinking, global business, we combine deep expertise with innovative technology to craft solutions that not only simplify the immigration process but also drive strategic success for our clients. Whether it is assisting multinational companies or helping people start new chapters in their lives, we are driven by the belief that global movement is the key to unlocking a world of possibilities. We are proud of our team spirit, and we like to reflect this in the way that we work and everything we do. Our Responsible Business Practices ("RBP") prioritize Fragomen's social responsibility through a cohesive strategy covering five key focus areas: Corporate Social Responsibility, Culture and Inclusion, Sustainability, Well\-Being and Pro Bono. Our RBP initiatives offer great opportunities for everyone at Fragomen to feel included, get involved and shape thoughtful initiatives and activities. For more information, please visit our website – https://www.fragomen.com/about/responsible\-business\-practices.html. *Fragomen is committed to promoting equal opportunities for all employees and applicants, regardless of race, ethnicity, heritage, gender, age, religion, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or intersex status.* *All offers and/or employment contracts are contingent upon the successful completion of the Firm’s pre\-employment screening process. This process may include verifying the candidate’s identity, confirming legal authorization to work in the offered position’s location, and conducting a comprehensive background check, where permitted by local regulations. We use limited AI‑assisted tools for administrative screening purposes only \- never for decision‑making. All hiring decisions are made by people. Applicants may have rights to information and explanations regarding the use of such tools, or request human review, as required by applicable regional laws.*

title: Industry accountant - Part-Qual
description: **The Role** Reporting directly to the Financial Controller and the CFO, you will be responsible for ensuring the integrity of the company\`s financial data and maintaining a rigorous compliance framework. You will play a key role in the monthly reporting cycle and act as a primary point of contact for tax and audit matters. **Key Responsibilities:** * **Tax Compliance \& Reporting:** Manage the preparation and timely filing of all tax returns, with a specific focus on VAT, Corporation Tax (CT), and Relevant Contracts Tax (RCT). Ensure full compliance with property\-related tax legislation. * **Monthly Financial Reporting:** Prepare accurate monthly management accounts, including P\&L analysis and comprehensive balance sheet reconciliations for group entities. * **Audit \& Internal Controls:** Lead the coordination of year\-end audits and internal audit reviews. Proactively implement corrective actions to strengthen the internal control environment. * **Operational Oversight:** Oversee weekly bank reconciliations and income audits to ensure robust cash flow management. * **Commercial Insight:** Support the budget and forecasting processes. Provide the CFO and senior management with commercial insights and drive cost\-control initiatives across various residential projects. * **Leadership \& Mentorship:** Provide guidance and support to junior finance team members and graduates, fostering a culture of continuous improvement. **Requirements:** * **Qualification:** Part\-qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, or CIMA) currently pursuing professional exams, with at least 2\-3 years of industry accounting experience. * **Technical Knowledge:** Strong understanding of accounting principles (IFRS/FRS 102\) and a keen interest in Irish tax law. * **Experience:** Previous experience in a fast\-paced finance environment. While a background in residential construction or property is an advantage, we welcome candidates from diverse sectors (e.g., hospitality or professional services) who possess strong transferable skills. * **Skills:** Exceptional analytical ability, high attention to detail, and the communication skills necessary to interact with stakeholders at all levels. * **Drive:** A self\-starter who takes initiative and is eager to improve existing processes.

title: Intermediate PowerApps Developer
description: **INTERMEDIATE POWER PLATFORM DEVELOPER** - Role Start Date: As soon as possible - Accenture Client: Public Sector Client, Dublin, Ireland - Reports To: Accenture/Avanade Power Platform/D365 Technical Lead - Job Type: Full\-time - Working Hours: 8 hours per day, Monday to Friday. This is negotiable depending on personal circumstances. - Experience Required: Around 4 years’ development experience, with 2\.5 years' experience developing on D365 / Microsoft Power Platform **Role Overview:** We’re looking for an **Intermediate Power Platform Developer** to support a major digital‑transformation project modernising legacy systems using Model‑Driven Apps, Dataverse, and D365\. In this role, you’ll build and customise Power Platform solutions, develop workflows with Power Automate, and support data migration from Lotus Notes to Dataverse. You’ll also contribute to integrations across Microsoft 365, Dynamics 365, and third‑party systems. Working closely with the Power Platform/D365 Technical Lead, developers, business analysts, and architects, you’ll help design scalable, secure applications that streamline business processes and follow best‑practice governance. This is a key role in delivering high‑quality solutions within a large public‑sector modernisation programme in Ireland. **Key Responsibilities:** - Design, develop, and implement Power Platform solutions utilising Model\-Driven Apps, Dataverse, Canvas Apps, and related tools. - Create and customise Dataverse entities, views, forms, and relationships to meet business requirements. - Develop and maintain plugins, custom workflows, and PowerApps Component Framework (PCF) controls to enhance functionality. - Integrate and configure WebResources (HTML, JavaScript, CSS) into Power Platform applications for customised user interfaces. - Work closely with business stakeholders to gather and analyse requirements, proposing effective solutions using Power Platform. - Collaborate with functional consultants and project teams to ensure high\-quality solutions are delivered. - Optimise performance and ensure solutions are scalable, maintainable, and aligned with best practices. - Troubleshoot and resolve issues related to Power Platform customisations and integrations. - Ensure compliance with company policies, industry standards, and security protocols in all custom development. **Qualifications:** - Strong experience developing Power Apps, especially Model‑Driven Apps, with a solid understanding of Dataverse architecture and customisation. - Proficient in C\#, JavaScript, and HTML/CSS for plugins, custom workflows, and Web Resources. - Skilled in Power Automate for building workflows and optimising business processes. - Experience with PCF and integrations using APIs, Azure services, and REST. - Excellent problem‑solving and communication skills, comfortable working in fast‑paced, agile, cross‑functional environments. **Preferred Skills:** - Experience with Microsoft Dynamics 365 (particularly Field Service or Customer Service modules) and its integration with Power Platform. - Familiarity with Azure DevOps, ALM, and source control processes. - Certification in Power Platform or Dynamics 365 (e.g., PL\-400, PL\-200\) is highly desirable. **What’s in it for you?** At Accenture Ireland, in addition to a competitive base salary, you will also have an extensive benefits package which includes pension, private health insurance, discounted gym membership, emergency caring facilities, and 2 days’ leave per year for charitable work of your choice! Flexible working arrangements can be discussed depending on the position. **Does this appeal to you? If so, we wish to hear from you.** Please advise if you have any particular requirements, so we can look into arranging reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process. You can contact us at accessIErecruitment@accenture.com Please also note that Accenture is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all sections of society and does not discriminate on grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, citizenship, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation, or gender identity.

title: Internship Opportunities 2026
description: **At Cadence, we hire and develop leaders and innovators who want to make an impact on the world of technology.** Cadence is a pivotal leader in electronic design, building upon more than 30 years of computational software expertise. The company applies its underlying Intelligent System Design strategy to deliver software, hardware and IP that turn design concepts into reality. Cadence customers are the world’s most innovative companies, delivering extraordinary electronic products from chips to boards to systems for the most dynamic market applications including consumer, hyperscale computing, 5G communications, automotive, aerospace industrial and health. Cadence also offer everything else you would expect from a world\-class technology organization and a Fortune 100 company, which is ranked as one of the best places to work worldwide: https://fortune.com/best\-companies/2020/cadence Cadence Cork are recruiting key talent across several Universities to join our Internship Programme for 2026\. If you are passionate about technology and would love to learn and grow in a cross\-geographic and multi\-cultural organization, developing next\-generation software, then Cadence Cork is for you. As part of your experience with Cadence, you will join a new class of professionals in our Career Catalyst Program. Through this program, you’ll receive special opportunities to develop your leadership capabilities, learn from Cadence executives and other industry leaders, and socialize and network with your peers. ***At Cadence, we hire and develop leaders and innovators who want to make an impact on the world of technology.*** **Job Title: Intern Opportunities 2026 (Software / Hardware Design)** **Location: Cork/Galway/Dublin** **Job Overview** We aim to make you an integral part of the team by providing training, mentorship, and encouragement of your creative talents. Our goal is to equip students with practical work experience. We understand that it is best to learn by doing. We will provide tasks that enable you to learn while making meaningful technical contributions. As part of the Cadence Internship program, you will get the opportunity to support different Business Units across the following functions including; * Digital Design Signoff * Customer IC / Analog / RF Design * System Analysis – including Thermal Design Management, EM solvers ad CFD Analysis * PCB Design and Analysis * System Design and Verification **The wealth of knowledge, experience, learning and autonomous culture of the team makes it a great team to work with.** **Job Qualifications** * Currently enrolled as BS or MS or Phd student majoring in Mathematics, Physics, Computer Science / Electrical / Electronic Engineering / Digital Humanities \& Information Technology or similar. * Looking for strong C\+\+, algorithms, and software development / scripting skills. * Understanding of analog/RF/custom IC design and verification practices is a strong plus. * Exposure to Cadence Virtuoso Layout and physical verification tools is a plus. * Team player, driven, self\-motivated and autonomous. * Excellent communication, presentation and customer service skills. * Problem Solving skills. **Additional Information** Cadence is committed to equal employment opportunity and employment equity throughout all levels of the organization. We strive to attract a qualified and diverse candidate pool and encourage diversity and inclusion in the workplace. **We’re doing work that matters. Help us solve what others can’t.**

title: Jnr MI Developer
description: Overview: **Jnr MI Developer (Entry Level)** **Salary:** R25,000 \- R35,000 depending on experience / qualifications **Location:** Home based **Department:** WFM and MI **Reports to**: Director of Business Insights **Contract:** Permanent **Closing date: (TBC)** **Job Overview** The role of the Jnr MI \& Data Developer is to provide support to our Lead Developers with data, reporting and insight that supports both the creation of robust resource plans and effective performance management within the Operation. This support to the operational team will largely be driven through the production of bespoke data, insight and analysis. This insight, delivered to senior stakeholders, will be used to improve forecasting, eliminate process inefficiencies and maximise the output and success of the planning and operational teams. The support to our call centres and operations is through daily and weekly reporting at a client/site level on operational performance including; * Call queue performance * Staffing and adherence performance * Shrinkage and absence analysis * Early Life and Glide Path performance * Agent KPIs and insight, such as AHT, Resolve Rate, Transfer Rate, NPS, Quality Measures, and all other measures bespoke to the client requirements. This is a key role in being able to highlight areas of opportunity within the performance of the team as a whole by providing agreed KPI’s in a timely manner with supporting commentary and insight. Key Stakeholders: * Operational Team * Workforce Planning * Best Practice * Transformation Team * Support Function Leads * Clients **Key Responsibilities and Accountabilities** *(may perform other duties as requested not specifically addressed in this document)* * Deliver insight on current and historic performance * Deliver insight on planning effectiveness and performance efficiency * Maintain and develop our internal reports, databases and data structures to ensure they are fit for purpose * Maintain and develop our internal planning tools in excel and other formats * Deliver new reporting through PowerBI as part of TPs global strategy * Working closely with the operational teams to ensure that the plans are well defined, clearly communicated and executed well * Conduct ad\-hoc analysis on performance variance, and improvements to planned forecasts or schedules * Develop and improve the presentation, relevance and effectiveness of agreed operational KPI’s * Provide analytical support for WFM, Best Practice and Operations * Administration keeping all system information accurately updated * Ensure presentations are fit for purpose and the audience * Manage the collation of effectiveness measures across Planning and Service Delivery * Steer as part of the wider team our direction and priorities **Main Job Requirements and Required Skills** * Understanding of SQL / MSQL query building and data management. * Strong excel skills including VBA preferable. * Business Objects, Azure, PowerBI experience or similar desirable * Strong analytical and data mining skills.. * Previous experience of data analysis and/or reporting within a call center environment. * Excellent knowledge of Microsoft applications, especially Excel and PowerPoint. * The ability to create complex reporting models. * The ability to work under pressure to tight deadlines. * A proven attention to detail. * Commercial awareness and customer focus. **Who are we?** We are experts in people interactions, and this gives us the edge in delivering superior customer experience in every contact. We constantly invest in research and development and get a deeper understanding of customer’s thoughts, behaviours and needs, across different countries, industries, channels, and generations. Our knowledge, technology, and market expertise, built through years of experience, allow us to successfully provide unique interactions all over the world. **What we offer** * Comprehensive initial training. * Recognition schemes * On\-going coaching and development and genuine opportunities to develop your career with the company’s management programmes * Discounts on top retail brands * 28 days’ holiday inclusive of bank holidays, rising to 30 from year 2 * Pension Scheme * Sports \& Social Scheme * Refer a friend and Earn scheme – Up to £900 for each individual referred * Discounted holidays, flights and hotels * Discounted utility bills * Discounted cinema tickets *This is a full\-time permanent role. Applicants must hold a valid and sufficiently long permission to work in Ireland to enable them to perform the role on an ongoing basis. Where work permission is due to expire shortly, the Company may require evidence of a pending renewal application to progress an offer.* *\[This role is not eligible for employment permit sponsorship. Applicants must already have, and maintain, a valid permission to work in Ireland for the duration of the role.]*

title: Jobshare - Security Engineer - Part time Jobshare
description: **JOB DESCRIPTION** This is an exciting opportunity where we’re exploring local Security Engineering talent who need flexibility, with the potential to present qualified candidates to managers for part\-time or job\-share roles. Part\-time hours where two people share the responsibilities. JPMorgan Chase \& Co. (NYSE: JPM) is a top financial services firm with over $2 trillion in assets, operating in more than 60 countries. As a leader in investment banking and various financial services, the firm’s Cybersecurity \& Technology Controls (CTC) group partners with all business lines to enhance cybersecurity, access management, and controls. The CTC group focuses on enabling business operations while ensuring protection and resilience. This role is part of the CTC Product Security team, aligned with the Azure Cloud Enablement (ACE) program. As a Cloud Security Engineer at JPMorgan Chase within the Cybersecurity \& Technology Controls (CTC) group, your primary responsibility will be to ensure that Public Cloud is adopted in a secure and compliant manner. You will play an important role in identifying and managing risk related issues and actions with respective technology. You will have an eye for detail and an ability to see the big picture across security issues. **Job Responsibilities:** * Support the execution and enhancement of a long term information risk and control strategy designed to keep the information assets of the public cloud secure. * Deliver risk based assessments of secure technology controls relating to cloud services, cloud platforms and architectural components. * Support business technology teams to understand firm control requirements and implementations across a broad range of cloud architectures. * Perform security reviews of infrastructure\-as\-code for cloud platform development. * Contribute to documentation and agile processes in support of security programs. * Interface with wider CTC teams ensuring platform integration with security operations, threat intelligence, IAM and network security. **Required Qualifications:** * Formal training or certification on Information Security concepts and proficient applied experience * Eagerness to collaborate in a team, and comfortable in both virtual and office environments. * Self\-disciplined, self\-managed, self\-motivated and strong sense of ownership, urgency, and drive. * Proficient verbal and written communication skills, including the ability to effectively participate in discussions and meetings with internal management, peer groups, regulators and senior stakeholders. * Ability to prioritize and work under stringent timelines. **Preferred Skills:** * Keen desire to understand and secure public cloud technology. * AWS, Azure or Google Cloud certifications would be an advantage. * Hands on experience of developing, engineering or architecting within a public cloud environment would be an advantage. * Experience engineering with Terraform or infrastructure\-as\-code would be an advantage. * Understanding of DevOps or CI/CD concepts would be an advantage. Your Pathway to Jobsharing at JPMorgan – IT’S A 2 BRAINER! At JPMorgan we are passionate about supporting different ways of working to support our talent in the flexibility they need. We know that Jobshare is a fantastic way to hire talent for the firm that offers both the flexibility that you need whilst providing the consistency that our business requires. Jobshare is 2 people working part time hours with full time powers. This is an exciting opportunity where we’re exploring local Security Engineering talent who need flexibility, with the potential to present qualified candidates to managers for part\-time or job\-share roles. Part\-time hours; where two people share the responsibilities for one role. This is a **pipeline** role exploring **Jobshare o**pportunity for 19 hours per week. **ABOUT US** J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world’s most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first\-class business in a first\-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long\-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives. We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation. **ABOUT THE TEAM** Our professionals in our Corporate Functions cover a diverse range of areas from finance and risk to human resources and marketing. Our corporate teams are an essential part of our company, ensuring that we’re setting our businesses, clients, customers and employees up for success.

title: Junior Associate - Employment
description: **The opportunity** We are looking for a junior Associate to join our growing team in Dublin. You would be working with our Employment Team and International Employment Group on a mix of contentious, noncontentious and corporate support work. **The team** Working with large and small corporations across all industry\-sectors impacted by technology, we offer clients a comprehensive range of legal advice across the full spectrum of employment law. Our expertise includes: Crossborder projects; Workforce restructuring and reductions; Employment support on corporate transactions; Senior level appointments and terminations; Complex disputes; Representation and resolution of claims before the Workplace Relations Commission and Irish courts; Outsourcing; Benefits and incentives; Performance management; Workplace investigations; Global mobility; Policies, procedures and in\-house documentation; and Protection of confidential information. The new team in Dublin consists of one partner and one senior associate and your hire is a key step in our growth strategy as we scale up to meet client demand. The team sits within a growingDublin office that is expanding since its opened in June 2022\. The team works closely with colleagues across the International Employment Group and there are opportunities to travel to these offices. Download

title: Junior BIM Engineer
description: Junior BIM Engineer, Ballycoolin Business Park, Dublin Apply Now Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions. We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Key Responsibilities Of The Role** * Responsible for production of 3D Construction models from base information – PID’s, Scan of facility and Cloud data. * Production of GA’s and detailing sheets. * Production of 3D design drawings, prefabrication isometrics and details. * Provision of construction detailing on the systems to enable the automatic production of isometrics and BOM. * Design and detailing of services support systems. * The production of builders works schedules and associated drawings. * Development of 3D co\-ordinated services and structure layouts for clash control. * Clash management on all systems. * Ensure integration of models into federated model for overall co\-ordination. * Development of mechanical \& electrical systems and designs, co\-ordinated building services layouts, management of drawing registers and standards. * Development of models and reports in line with project scheduling requirements. * Problem solving and resolution of co\-ordination and design road blocks. * Adherence to Mercury’s CAD and BIM manuals and processes. * Collaboration with other trades \& tool managers and project managers. * Assistance in the overall development of the BIM protocols and systems. * Liaison with Mercury site staff in interpreting and amending models. * Travel to sites (both in Ireland and abroad) may be required. * Attend project meeting as required, protecting Mercury’s interests as required. * Field surveying and site verification. * Remain fully up to date with the drawing management. * Complete material procurement schedules. * Ensure compliance and adherence to the Clients ER and EIR’s. * Ensure compliance and adherence to Mercury’s EHS Policies and systems. * Ensure QA/QC compliance. * All other duties associated with this role. **Essential Criteria For The Role** * 1\-2 years’ experience in a BIM or Engineering environment. * Excellent organizational skills. * Ability to work as part of a team. * Ability to work on own initiative. * Excellent negotiation, problem solving and decision\-making skills. * Excellent IT skills. * Excellent MS Office skills. ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer*** **.** Apply Now

title: Junior Consultant - Training Provided
description: Are you an ambitious professional looking to make a significant impact in a new and dynamic field? If you have a passion for people and thrive in fast\-paced environments, we have an exciting opportunity for you! * **Unlimited Earning Potential: Fast\-Paced \& Rewarding Environment** * **Clear and Transparent Career Development – Progress to Leadership** * **Personalised Training – Use your Sales skills in a new exciting way** **What can you expect day to day?** As an Associate Recruitment Consultant, you'll have the freedom to do things differently, supported by colleagues with a wealth of experience, empowering you to learn and grow. No day is the same here at Access, and you will gain experience across a varied range of skills including: * Building relationships with clients and candidates * Managing vacancies from start to finish * Screening, interviewing and shortlisting candidates in the healthcare industry Here at Access, you really can influence your own earning potential, by getting out what you put in. Your energy and dedication will be rewarded in a culture built on celebrating success. **What we are looking for** : * 1 – 2 years of professional experience (any industry can be considered) – No previous recruitment experience is needed. * Passion to succeed and be part of a fast\-paced inclusive environment * Relationship\-focused with the confidence to build strong networks * Collaborative in spirit with a growth mindset to develop a long\-lasting career **Benefits \& Perks** * Basic Salary \+ Uncapped Commission (Average OTE: Year 1 €45k, Year 2 €75k) * Internal Learning and development, helping support and train alongside the support of your manager * Clear path for progression: until 12 promotions available all the way to Equity Partner \+ Leadership Academy programme for managers in the making (all leaders grow inside) * Paid sabbatical * Wellness programme (e.g. time management, skills development, health talks, advise and activities, stress management) * Industry\-leading training programme with 200\+ training modules – Become a Certified Recruitment Consultant * Fantastic team culture, very collaborative and team socials If you are curious enough to know more, all you need to do is send us a current CV! francisca.simoes@accesshealthcare.ie ***Please note that only EU Citizens \& Stamp 4 applications will be considered for this role.***

title: Junior Consultant – Full training provided
description: * **Expert training from day one \- start strong, learn fast, and crush it from the jump.** * **Constant growth \- never settle, always level up, and keep your skills unstoppable.** * **Certified \& credible \- earn your stripes, stand out, and own your field.** Are you stuck in customer service or sales, hitting targets but seeing no real growth? Ready for a career where your results actually fuel your success. At **Access Healthcare** , we specialize in helping driven professionals from all kinds of backgrounds launch successful careers in recruitment. Our team is growing, and we’re looking for ambitious people ready to thrive in a fast\-paced, results\-driven environment and become Recruitment Consultants with us. **Why choose Access Healthcare for your career move?** * **From Zero to Expert:** 200\+ training modules, 1:1 coaching, and continuous development, you’ll never feel stuck. * **Continuous Development:** You’ll never feel stagnant. With nonstop training, you’ll keep developing your skills and growing your career. * **Get Certified:** Become a Certified Recruitment Consultant and build credibility that opens doors. **What You Will Need** * At least 2 year of experience in a sales\-focused role (e.g., Sales Rep, Sales Associate, Account Executive, Business Development, Inside sales, Tele sales representative, Sales Manager, Customer Success Manager, etc.) * An ambitious, growth\-driven mindset * A bachelor’s degree or equivalent is a plus but not required. **Total Compensation (Average Performer)** * Year 1: **€45,000\+** * Year 2: **€75,000\+** * Base salary: DOE * Commission: Uncapped, unlimited earning potential * Top performers earn significantly above these figures Ready for a new, exciting opportunity? Contact Maria de Gouveia at maria.degouveia@accesshealthcare.ie or call \+353 1 572 1202 to learn more about this thrilling opportunity. Take your career to new heights!

title: Junior Industrial Designer
description: **Company Description** We are seeking a talented **Junior\-Level** **Industrial Designer** to join our fantastic team of researchers, designers, engineers, and prototypers at our studio in **Co. Wicklow, Ireland** to help create future products for some of the world’s most exciting consumer, professional and healthcare brands. Suitable applicants will be passionate about delivering meaningful design solutions that help our clients create world\-leading, and best\-selling products and experiences. **Job Description** You will: * Help design and develop consumer electronics and similar devices for mass production * Participate in research and mine for insight to inspire solutions * Build compelling design narratives that convey the brand and audience you are designing for * Create outstanding product concepts across a broad spectrum of industries * Possess a strong aesthetic sensibility and a great eye for detail * Design holistic user experiences * Have a sustainability mindset when developing new concept ideas * Continually hone your creative, technical and communication skills * Confidently move between craft and digital tools to optimise creative workflow * Collaborate with an open mind with colleagues and clients * Lead with creativity and act with urgency * Drive to design at the highest level for our clients and their customers **Our Team** We offer you the opportunity to work and grow in a dynamic and supportive team environment. We are a forward\-looking team, built to tackle what’s next. Our ambition is to create solutions that drive impact, define categories and make a difference for people, business and the planet. You will be guided to continually imbue these qualities in your work. With full R\&D facilities and a highly experienced team you will have the equipment, encouragement, and expertise to fully develop your concepts from early crafted visions through to fully working prototypes and design for manufacture. Our work environment is second to none \- a fully equipped 1,500 sq m. combined studio, workshops and labs designed to maximise experimentation, creativity, and fast progress. PA’s Irish studio is located in the beautiful surroundings of Co. Wicklow – ‘the Garden of Ireland’ – known for hiking, cycling, trail running and other outdoor activities. The office is five minutes from Bray’s seafront where people enjoy walks, sea swimming, surfing, cafes, and restaurants, and 30 minutes from Dublin City Centre, for those looking for more vibrant evenings, weekends and nightlife. **Qualifications** * Excellent portfolio of industrial design experience showcasing your skills, thinking, and methodology * Bachelor’s degree in industrial design * Ideally 2 years professional experience in design consultancy/agency * Fluent and confident with hand sketching and craft modelling * Exceptional aesthetic sensibility * Empathetic to user needs, motivations, and behaviours * Thorough understanding of three\-dimensional design and skilled with Rhino/Solid Works and Keyshot (or similar). * Proficiency across multiple design tools you will use in projects (e.g. Miro, Figma, Adobe Creative suite, Teams etc.) * Experienced at tuning in to brand values and crafting compelling stories * Experience working with consumer and healthcare brands is a positive but not essential * Excellent communication and presentation skills with a strong ability to articulate and convince others of your ideas and strategies * Solid understanding of industry trends and advancements in design and creativity * Good understanding of engineering and manufacturing requirements * Knowledgeable and passionate about emerging technologies (AI, Machine learning, Generative AI, XR, etc.) and its impact on design solutions. **Additional Information** Life At PA encompasses our peoples' experience at PA. It's about how we enrich peoples’ working lives by giving them access to unique people and growth opportunities and purpose led meaningful work. Our purpose guides how we work with our clients and our teams, and support our communities, to deliver insight and impact, solving the world’s most complex challenges. We're focused on building a workplace that values human difference and diverse mindsets, and a culture of inclusion and equality that unlocks the potential in our people so everyone can be their best self. Find out more about Life at PA here. We are dedicated to supporting the physical, emotional, social and financial well\-being of our people. Check out some of our extensive benefits: * Health and lifestyle perks accompanying private healthcare for you and your family * 25 days annual leave (plus a bonus half day on Christmas Eve) with the opportunity to buy 5 additional days * Generous company pension scheme * Opportunity to get involved with community and charity\-based initiatives * Annual performance\-based bonus * PA share ownership * Tax efficient benefits (cycle to work, give as you earn) We’re committed to advancing equality. We recruit, retain, reward and develop our people based solely on their abilities and contributions and without reference to their age, background, disability, genetic information, parental or family status, religion or belief, race, ethnicity, nationality, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity (or expression), political belief, veteran status, or any other range of human difference brought about by identity and experience. We welcome applications from underrepresented groups. Adjustments or accommodations \- Should you need any adjustments or accommodations to the recruitment process, at either application or interview, please contact us on recruitmentenquiries@paconsulting.com

title: Junior M&E Quantity Surveyor - Kildare
description: We have an exciting opportunity for a Junior M\&E Quantity Surveyor to join Suir Engineering Ltd. This position is for a semiconductor project at Leixlip, Co. Kildare. Here in Suir Engineering we are a leading international provider of innovative Mechanical, Electrical and Instrumentation Engineering Solutions. Our core sectors are Energy, Power and Renewables, Pharma, Food \& Beverage, and Data Centres. We provide best in class contracting solutions with in\-house, flexible resources capable of delivering a personable and consistent service to our customers. Over the past 40 years we have delivered projects for Irish and multinational clients across the globe. We are currently expanding significantly, and are delivering large scale projects across Ireland, the U.K, Germany, Denmark and Sweden. We recognise that continued business success depends on our commitment to and development of our people. We are determined to create a diverse and equal workforce and to ensure our company appeals to the best and brightest people. We wish for all employees to realise their full potential and have the opportunity to grow and develop during their career with Suir. We are also working continuously to promote a culture of positive wellbeing through our wellbeing programmes and supports to employees and their families through our Employee Assistance Programme and the Lighthouse Club. **What you will be doing?** * Management and ownership of the commercial and contractual requirements through the life cycle of the project. * Responsibility for interface Client Commercial/Contract Team. * Project Procurement including Sub\-contractors. * Conduct cost and risk analysis. * Review all incoming correspondence from Employers, Design Team and /or subcontractors, to identify contractually critical items and to initiate replies. * Manage commercial aspects of subcontractors. * Provide project costing, margin \& cashflow reports. * Manage project change order procedures and agree costs with client. **Is this the job for me?** * Suitable Candidates must have a 3rd level qualification in Construction Economics / Quantity Surveying and 2\-3 years’ experience working as a Mechanical or Electrical Quantity Surveyor * Excellent oral and written communication skills. * Previous experience working in the electrical/mechanical field * Ability to prioritize workload according to deadlines together with a high attention to detail. * Ability to influence colleagues at all levels. * Enjoy working as part of a high performing team. **In Return From Suir Engineering** * 23 days annual leave * Company Pension contributions, Life and Disability cover * Private Health Insurance contribution * Educational Assistance, CPD, Chartership supports and upskilling opportunities * Opportunities for international assignments to work on one of our European projects in Sweden, Denmark, or Germany. Our way of working is called the Suir Way, designed to help us on a journey of continuous improvement. * Our people are empowered to make changes to ensure a quality install through effective ways of working. * Our people have a voice and feel empowered. * We have an in\-built culture of innovation, learning and continuous improvement. * Value is delivered every step of the journey. Our efficient processes deliver value. * We have a standard, consistent and fully integrated health, safety, environment, and quality system within the business. *Suir Engineering are an equal opportunities employer, we value our greatest asset …. our People.*

title: Junior Marketing Specialist
description: **Description** **About Sadie** Sadie is transforming how restaurants engage with their customers using AI\-powered voice technology. Our intelligent voice agent, Sadie, answers phone calls 24/7 to take bookings, manage reservations, answer FAQs, promote specials, and drive upsells—all without putting pressure on front\-of\-house staff. Sadie integrates with the world’s leading booking engines and POS systems to deliver a seamless guest experience and operational efficiency. We’re live in over 1,000 restaurants across Canada, the US, UK, Germany, and Australia, and we’re just getting started. **The Role** We are looking for a motivated and detail\-oriented Junior Marketing Specialist to support our growth across the UK and Ireland market. Based in Ireland, you will play a key role in executing Sadie’s marketing initiatives, helping drive brand awareness, customer engagement, and demand generation. This role is ideal for someone early in their marketing career who is eager to learn, experiment, and grow within a fast\-paced, high\-growth tech company. **What You’ll Do** * Support the development and execution of UK and Ireland\-focused marketing campaigns across digital, social, email, and events. * Assist in creating compelling content, including social posts, landing pages, newsletters, case studies, and sales collateral. * Monitor campaign performance and provide insights through regular reporting and analytics. * Collaborate with the sales and customer success teams to develop market\-specific materials and support outreach initiatives. * Conduct market research to identify trends, competitor activities, and opportunities for brand positioning. * Help manage Sadie’s presence at trade shows, webinars, and industry events. * Maintain and update Sadie’s website, marketing tools, and CRM entries as needed. * Contribute creative ideas to enhance marketing strategy and improve customer engagement. **What We’re Looking For** * 1–2 years of marketing experience (internships or co\-op experience welcome). * Strong communication and writing skills, with a keen eye for detail and storytelling. * Familiarity with social media platforms, digital marketing tools, and basic analytics. * Comfortable working in a fast\-paced environment with shifting priorities. * Self\-starter with strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple tasks. * Interest in hospitality, AI technology, or SaaS is a strong plus. * Experience with tools like HubSpot, Canva, Google Analytics, or similar platforms is an advantage. **Why You’ll Love Working at Sadie** * Join a fast\-growing startup that’s redefining how restaurants operate. * Work with a supportive, ambitious, and collaborative team across multiple markets. * Opportunities for rapid career growth and hands\-on learning. * Competitive compensation and benefits. * Flexible, remote\-friendly work environment. \#Sadie

title: Junior Mortgage Broker
description: We are hiring a **Mortgage Broker** for a Leading Mortgage Brokerage firm based in Dublin City Centre! This is an exceptional and rewarding opportunity for an ambitious Mortgage Advisor who is passionate about delivering unforgettable customer service and are eager to progress in their career. This is a **Hybrid Working Opportunity** offering great work \- life balance. **The Role:** * Help clients finance the purchase of properties and switch existing mortgages * Work within a team of advisors in a fast\-paced environment * Manage a portfolio of clients, providing advice to new clients and ongoing support throughout the mortgage completion process * Ensure accurate data entry at all times * Identify the best mortgage solutions * Manage and track related paperwork, including bank statements and lender applications * Liaise and negotiate with banks and lenders to secure the most suitable loan options for clients financial affordability **Ideal Candidate:** * At least 2\+ years' experience in providing mortgage advice in a bank or a brokerage firm * QFA (Qualified Financial Adviser) designation * Good communication skills and confident ability * Strong understanding of the full end to end mortgage process * Target\-driven with a proven track record of achieving goals **Benefits / Perks:** * Competitive Salary with Uncapped Commission * 25 Days Annual Leave * Pension Contribution * Death in Service * Income Protection * Hybrid Working Please apply through the portal OR you can send your CV to khickey@eirkoo.ie if you are interested in this opportunity. You can also phone (01\) 699 1200 and ask for Kate if you would prefer to have a confidential chat.

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: Dublin \- Dublin \- Ireland **About The Role** As a CBRE Project Management Analyst, you be responsible for providing administrative support to the project management team. This includes documentation preparation, escalation for commitment, financial modeling, financial reconciliations, and reporting. This job is part of the Project Management function. They are responsible for the management of projects from initiation through completion. **What You’ll Do** * Act as systems administrator of the internet\-based project tracking system, project websites and job cost accounting system. * Respond to internal and external customer inquiries concerning performance reports and project tracking system data. Escalate to management as needed. * Assist the team with the execution, monitoring and adherence of project administrative practices and policies. * Provide internal and external customer training on software applications. * Assist with close\-out procedures. * Train new administrative assistants and temporary employees on policies and administration. * Prepare routine and special client reports as required. * Have some knowledge of standard principles with limited practical experience in applying them. * Lead by example and model behaviors that are consistent with CBRE RISE values. * Impact the quality of own work. * Work within standardized procedures and practices to achieve objectives and meet deadlines. * Exchange straightforward information, ask questions, and check for understanding. **What You’ll Need:** * Bachelor's Degree preferred with up to 3 years of relevant experience. In lieu of a degree, a combination of experience and education will be considered. * Ability to use existing procedures to solve standard problems. * Experience with analyzing information and standard practices to make judgments. * In\-depth knowledge of Microsoft Office products. Examples include Word, Excel, Outlook, etc. * Organizational skills with a strong inquisitive mindset. * Intermediate math skills. Ability to calculate difficult figures such as percentages, fractions, and other financial\-related calculations. **Service line:** PJM Segment

title: Junior Site Engineer
description: **Junior Site Engineer – Civil Infrastructure** A leading civil engineering contractor is seeking a **Graduate / Junior Site Engineer** to join their growing team across projects in **Meath** and surrounding areas. This is an excellent opportunity for a final\-year student or recent graduate to gain hands\-on experience on large\-scale civil infrastructure and public realm projects. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist with the delivery of civil infrastructure projects including roads, cycle lanes, drainage, and public realm upgrades * Support site setting out, surveying, and quality control * Monitor project progress and report to senior engineers and project managers * Coordinate with subcontractors and site teams * Ensure compliance with health \& safety standards and company procedures * Assist in preparing site reports and documentation **Requirements** * Degree in Civil Engineering (or related discipline) – graduating May/June or recently qualified * **Must have completed some form of work placement or internship during studies (even short\-term), ideally within civil engineering, construction, or utilities** * Strong communication and organisational skills * Willingness to work on\-site across multiple locations * Basic knowledge of AutoCAD or Civil 3D is an advantage * Full driving licence preferred **What’s on Offer** * **€40k–€45k starting salary** * Exposure to major civil and public realm projects * Structured mentoring and career development * Clear progression pathway within a leading contractor **To apply:** If you are a motivated Graduate / Junior Engineer looking to kick\-start your career, please send your CV or contact Amy Maguire for a confidential discussion. Email: a.maguire@gpcireland.com *GPC will not send your CV to clients without prior discussion and consent.*

title: Junior Software Developer
description: **Role Purpose** The Junior Software Developer will work closely with our application teams to develop, modify, and support our business applications \& systems. The software developer will be responsible for handling all aspects related to the provision and maintenance of software solutions in HRI. Reporting to the Business Applications Manager, the Junior Software Developer will assist in the development and maintenance of .NET applications such as Web Content Management System (CMS), RESTful API development and SharePoint development, working closely with senior developers to deliver high\-quality software solutions. This includes taking technical responsibility for maintaining \& supporting existing business applications \& systems, working on own initiative to find optimum solutions, in addition to ensuring that software systems are optimized for performance, and that the integrity and security is maintained to high standards, using industry best practice. **Key Responsibilities** * To keep up to date with best practice in Applications Development specifically Microsoft technologies using .Net with C\# as the development language and SQL Server as the database management system. * To apply a range of technical competence to all assigned work in terms of: * C\#, ASP.NET Technology stack (.NET CORE, MVC), Microsoft SQL Server, T\-SQL * ASP.NET Core web application development * Web Content Management System (CMS) application development * REST API development * SharePoint Development * To undertake specific application assignments and deliver these in accordance with the software development lifecycle project management best practice. * Provide support for complex \& critical business applications specifically the CMS web applications and SharePoint Sites and deal with issues as they arise. * Use technical and functional specifications to translate user requirements. * Support, advise, clarify requirements, and collaborate with internal customers on development projects. * Resolve technical issues through debugging, research, and investigation. * Support all other IT members as and when required to meet the department's quality and service objectives. * Keep abreast of best practices \& appropriately network to broaden self\-experience to be of business benefit \- driving continuous improvement in our IT and Service delivery. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams. **Skills and Experience required:** * Relevant Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science or related discipline is essential * 1 \- 2 years of commercial experience as a software developer building application solutions using C\# .NET * Solid understanding of OOP (Object Oriented Programming) and computer science foundations * Experience with client\-side web development technologies such as HTML, CSS, JavaScript, jQuery * Troubleshooting (debugging) and performance tuning skills * Pursues with energy, drive. * Ability to problem solve and use initiative to generate effective solutions. * Strong analytic and problem\-solving skills; ability to learn quickly and clarify poorly defined problems. * Strong interpersonal and communication skills * Strong client\-facing skills are a must for this role where technical support is required. Desirable Skills and Experience: * Microsoft Certified Solutions Developer (MCSD) certification. * REST API knowledge. * Experience with Kentico CMS development would be hugely beneficial. * Experience with responsive frameworks (such as Bootstrap, Foundation etc.). * Experience with SharePoint Framework (SPFx) and SharePoint APIs. * Experience using Git. * Knowledge of Azure DevOps. HRI is an equal opportunities employer. Interview candidates will be provided with any necessary reasonable accommodations when called for interview.

title: Junior Software Engineer
description: **PLAY, GROW and WIN** To be a part of Virtuos means to be a creator. At Virtuos, we harness the latest technologies to make games better and more immersive than ever before. That is why we pride ourselves in constantly pushing the boundaries of possibility since our founding in 2004\. Virtuosi is a team of experts – people who have come together to share their mutual passion for making and playing games. People with the same enthusiasm for exploring new ideas and the constant drive to excel in their field. People who believe in earning success through dedication. At Virtuos, we are at the forefront of gaming, creating exciting new experiences daily. Join us to Play, Grow and Win – together. **ABOUT THE POSITION** Our studio is continuing to grow, and with new and exciting projects coming in, we are seeking a **Junior Software Engineer** for our studio located in Dublin, to join us in developing AAA games for some of the most renowned studios in the world. This position is ideal for people starting their career in games or have 1\-2 years of experience. * Implement and develop systems for NPC AI, UI, and general gameplay. * Deliver high\-performance, high\-quality, and well\-documented code. * Test and deploy your work on PC and consoles. * Build features for AAA games that run smoothly on a wide variety of consoles and platforms. * Use source control and bug reporting software. * Prioritize and execute tasks in a team environment. * Provide engineering support to our adjacent disciplines in art, animation, and design. * Strong C\+\+ skills. * Experience with Unreal Engine or a Custom Engine and good knowledge of its gameplay and AI systems. * Competent at 3D mathematics related to gaming. * At least one solid example of implementing a game via work experience, game jam or college project. * Excellent problem\-solving, organizational, and communication skills. * Familiar with profiling and debugging tools. * Clear passion for gaming. **Desirable** * Professional experience working with AI and Gameplay in Unreal. * Agile development experience. * Tool creation experience. * Familiarity with other coding languages such as C\#, Python, Groovy. Founded in 2004, Virtuos is one of the largest independent video game development companies. We are headquartered in Singapore with offices in Asia, Europe, and North America. Specializing in full\-cycle game development and art production, we have delivered high\-quality content for more than 1,500 console, PC, and mobile games. Our clients include 23 of the top 25 gaming companies worldwide. **About Our Team**Black Shamrock was founded in 2016 and acquired by the Virtuos Group in 2017\. The studio is Ireland’s largest PC and console game development studio and provides unique opportunities to work on multiple AAA titles. Our people are as diverse, exciting, creative, and dynamic as the projects that we work on. Black Shamrock is a place for talent to flourish and lifelong industry connections to begin. Our in\-house and full\-service capabilities include narrative, game, sound and level design, animation, art, rigging, engineering, and graphics, to name a few areas our skilled team members work on. At Black Shamrock, we take pride in helping put Dublin on the world map as the birthplace of award\-winning AAA games. * Being part of one of the largest and fastest\-growing game development studios in Ireland * Dynamic career paths contributing to some of the world’s biggest AAA titles in gaming * Opportunities to grow in a tight\-knit community with a people\-oriented culture * Healthy work\-life balance and flexible work arrangements * Comprehensive company healthcare plan * Occupational Pension Scheme (including employer contribution) * Global training and development plans \- study support, formal education, international experience, career planning and progression opportunities * Bank for paid sick leave and wellness leave * Subsidised leave * Relocation support * Taxsaver plans * Career and international mobility opportunities with Virtuos Internal Mobility Program People matter. Diverse opinions and experiences matter. At Virtuos, our talented teams are the cornerstone of our success, and we recognize that fostering and advocating for inclusivity is at the center of what we do best \- we make games better, together. Virtuos is proud to be an equal opportunity employer that embraces diversity of thought, expression, culture, and backgrounds.

title: Laboratory Apprentice - Carlow
description: **Job Description** An exciting opportunity to join our team as a microbiology laboratory apprentice. The laboratory apprenticeship is a full time opportunity to earn while you learn, this is an apprenticeship program with IBEC. This is a 2 year level 6 course \- (Higher Certificate in Science Laboratory Technician and a continuation option to be qualified as a Laboratory Analyst (level 7 Ordinary Bachelor of Science degree). it consists of both industry training and academia study using both theory and practical elements. Tasks carried out in the laboratory are in accordance with the course. **Bring energy, knowledge, innovation to carry out the following** * Perform assigned analytical testing with accuracy, completeness and compliance with the established procedures and specifications as directed by your Laboratory Team Lead/designee * Ensure compliance with GMP and EHS guidelines at all times. * Assisting laboratory analytical personnel supporting housekeeping activities and consumable stock management * Perform basic routine instrument calibrations and maintenance checks * Responsible for carrying out environmental monitoring and water sampling activities * Plan and organise sample shipments ensuring appropriate coordination with supply chain and external laboratory. * Ensure adherence to all current regulatory data integrity (ALCOA) requirements * Highlight any discrepancies deviations are non\-conformance in testing our work practices and support investigations as required * Provide assistance with laboratory audit preparation activities where required **What Educational Requirements/skills You Will Need** For entry into year 1 of the Level 6 Programme applicants must have the following * Minimum of Grade B3/O3 in PASS Maths and a minimum Grade D/O6 in four other subjects in the Leaving Certificate Exam or an approved equivalent * Or three years’ work experience in a relevant designated industrial activity \*Note This criteria exception is only applicable for mature students over the age of 23\. SOLAS will determine if the applicants previous experience is deemed as relevant industry experience * Achieves the required level of Aptitude Testing * Strong Interpersonal and communication skills We keep the patient at the very heart of all that we do and strive to find solutions and treatments for some of the world's most challenging healthcare needs. As an equal opportunity employer, we are proud to be a company that embraces the value of bringing diverse, talented, and committed people together. Please don't hesitate to contact the Talent Acquisition Advisor assigned to this role should you need any support during our recruitment process. **So, if you are ready to** Invent solutions to meet unmet healthcare needs, **please apply today.** **Required Skills** Accountability, Accountability, Analytical Testing, Calibration Management Software, Data Integrity, Environmental Monitoring, Environmental Monitoring Systems, Equipment Calibrations, GMP Compliance, Laboratory Analysis, Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS), Laboratory Operations, Laboratory Sciences, Laboratory Testing, Manufacturing Compliance, Microbiology, Molecular Microbiology, PCR Testing, Quality Control Management, Quality Improvement Programs, Quality Management, Quality Process Development, Quality Standards, Root Cause Analysis (RCA), Safety Protocols {\+ 5 more} **Preferred Skills** Current Employees apply HERE Current Contingent Workers apply HERE **Search Firm Representatives Please Read Carefully** Merck \& Co., Inc., Rahway, NJ, USA, also known as Merck Sharp \& Dohme LLC, Rahway, NJ, USA, does not accept unsolicited assistance from search firms for employment opportunities. All CVs / resumes submitted by search firms to any employee at our company without a valid written search agreement in place for this position will be deemed the sole property of our company. No fee will be paid in the event a candidate is hired by our company as a result of an agency referral where no pre\-existing agreement is in place. title: Linux Kernel Engineer
description: At Apple, we craft experiences that have transformed entire industries. The diversity of our people and ideas fuels innovation in everything we do. Imagine what you could build here. Join our Linux Kernel Engineering team and play a central role in the reliability, performance, and evolution of Apple’s platforms. As a Linux Kernel Engineer, you will own the kernel lifecycle end to end, from CI infrastructure and automated validation to driver enablement, debugging, and production\-quality releases. You will partner closely with platform, infrastructure, and SRE teams to ensure every kernel we ship is stable, observable, and ready for real world workloads at scale. **Description** In this role, you will design, build, and operate CI and validation pipelines that compile, test, and release Linux kernels across multiple hardware platforms and Linux distributions. You will lead triage of kernel regressions, investigate crashes and performance issues, drive fixes to resolution, and qualify new hardware at the kernel layer. You will collaborate with engineers across Apple to port, maintain, and upstream device drivers, shepherd kernel changes into production, and continuously raise the bar on kernel quality, automation, and release discipline. Your work will have direct impact on the stability, performance, and debuggability of systems that power critical Apple services. If you enjoy deep kernel debugging, building robust CI systems, and owning kernel releases from development through production, we would like to hear from you. **Preferred Qualifications** Proven track record of upstream contributions to the Linux kernel project or similar upstreams. Bachelor of Computer Science or equivalent skills and work experience. **Minimum Qualifications** Experience developing or maintaining the Linux kernel or low\-level system software (e.g., kernel modules, device drivers, or core OS components). Proven ability to debug kernel level issues (panics, hangs, performance regressions) using tools such as perf, ftrace, bpftrace, crash, gdb, or similar. Deep understanding of kernel tuning for stability and performance across many subsystem domains. Hands\-on experience developing CI/CD systems integrating operating system components and managing kernel releases. Strong programming skills in C, plus proficiency in at least one of Rust, Go, Python, or Bash. Solid understanding of operating system fundamentals: processes, memory management, filesystems, networking, scheduling, interrupts, and hardware/driver interactions. Experience working in a RHEL based Linux environment, including RPM packaging and the RHEL kernel back port and release process. Strong collaboration and communication skills; comfortable working with cross\-functional teams (SRE, platform, hardware, security).","internalDetails":null,"eeoContent":null
